The Union Securities Co. of Spo-
kane has taken over the Security
State bank at PalousS on the basis
of a valuation of 100000. The
Union Securities Co. was formed
for the purposes of combining some
Is or 20 small banks in the Inland
Empire. 1t was largely backed by
men interested in the Old National
| bank.


Building permits for January in
creased six. in number and $23,035
in value over the same month in
1909 The number issued was 107
and the amount $29S,195.


Dick Boylen, who alleged that he
was the founder of the l. w. w.
and instituted the organization in
Spokane, last night startled the
police by declaring that the plan of
campaign followed for free speech
in Spokane was contrary to the
principles of the order and that if
he was on the ground at the time
he would have taken the platform
on the other side, championing the
cause of the police.


South Mantle Park addition, con-
tanning 20 acres, will be placed on
the market in the spring by James
c. Broad, R. l.. Webster and c. e.
Mallette. 1t joins Alamo Park ad-
dition on the south and runs east
from Grand to Perry streets.


The Spokane Transfer & Storage
Co. has increased it's capital stock
from $4,000 to $4,000. j. H. Nc
Allister and w. w. Belden are the
incorporators.


The season will open at Nata-
forum park Decoration day, May
so, under the personal supervision
of R. A. Wilson, assistant general
manager.


An official invitation to attend
the first worlds fair in the republic
of Argentine, to be held at Buenos
| Ayres next June and July, has been
ereceived by John T. Burns, secre-
Itary of the Dry Farming congress-
Mr.. Burns regrets that he must de-
cline the invitation.
